behavior_in_task_description.reason: All file paths, class names (Qwen2VLTextConfig, Qwen2_5_VLTextConfig, ColQwen2Config, ColModernVBertConfig, ModernVBertForMaskedLM), and the attribute/field names (tie_word_embeddings, config parameter type annotation) referenced in tests are explicitly mentioned in instruction.md's 'Files to investigate' and 'Expected behavior' sections.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes what is broken (tie_word_embeddings incorrectly defined, hack in __post_init__, missing type annotation) but does not specify exact line numbers to change or the precise code to write. The agent must still determine the exact edits.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: The tests check structural properties via AST (no tie_word_embeddings field in text configs, type annotation present on config param) and text presence checks for the hack removal. Any correct fix that removes the field, adds the annotation, and removes the hack would pass regardless of implementation details.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: test_downstream_no_tie_propagation_hack (truncated but visible pattern) checks source text for 'tie_word_embeddings' string presence rather than executing behavioral code. The core fail-to-pass tests (test_qwen2vl_text_no_own_tie_field, test_qwen2_5_vl_text_no_own_tie_field) use AST inspection rather than importing/calling the classes. test_modernvbert_init_type_hint also uses AST only. While some tests run subprocesses (ruff, check_copies), the fail-to-pass tests are all static/text-based with

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests check for the presence of tie_word_embeddings fields that exist in the original code and must be removed, check for a type annotation that is missing in the original code, and check for hack code that exists in the original. A stub/no-op change would not pass these tests; actual code modifications are required.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All three expected behaviors from instruction.md are tested: text sub-configs not defining tie_word_embeddings (test_qwen2vl_text_no_own_tie_field, test_qwen2_5_vl_text_no_own_tie_field), composite configs not having hack (test_downstream_no_tie_propagation_hack), and type annotation on ModernVBertForMaskedLM.__init__ (test_modernvbert_init_type_hint, test_modular_init_type_hint).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section comments (Gates pass_to_pass repo_tests, Gates pass_to_pass static, Fail-to-pass pr_diff), descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining what each test verifies and why the approach was chosen.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves code/config changes, not structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB schemas.

no_typos.reason: All file paths, class names, attribute names, and commands in instruction.md match the actual repository structure and test references without typos.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly names the exact files, exact class names, exact method names (__post_init__), and the exact attribute (tie_word_embeddings as a class-level dataclass field) to change. It also specifies removing the 'workaround hack in __post_init__' and adding 'type annotation on its config parameter'. This goes beyond describing what's broken and effectively localizes all changes precisely.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task involving Python source file edits, not configuration file edits in the agentmd-edits sense.
